UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user account in the MUTAMBUKE system, abstracting common details for both passengers and drivers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This ID is typically generated by the authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth userId).

fullName.description: The full name of the user as provided during registration.

phoneNumber.description: The user's contact phone number, essential for communication.

nationalId.description: The user's national identification number, required for verification purposes (e.g., for drivers).

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and as an optional login method.

role.description: The role of the user within the platform (e.g., 'passenger' or 'driver').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture, stored externally (e.g., Firebase Storage).

DriverProfile.title: DriverProfile
DriverProfile.description: Stores detailed information specific to a driver, complementing their UserProfile and managing their operational status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DriverProfile entity. This ID is identical to the 'id' of the associated UserProfile where the role is 'driver'.

userId.description: Reference to the associated UserProfile entity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 DriverProfile)

status.description: The current availability status of the driver (e.g., 'online', 'offline', 'on_trip').

currentLocation.description: A GeoPoint string (e.g., 'latitude,longitude') representing the driver's real-time geographic coordinates. This can be stored as a string or a GeoPoint type in Firestore for spatial queries, but for database agnosticism, a string is used here. Alternatively, could be an object with 'latitude' and 'longitude' fields.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the driver's status or location was last updated.

drivingLicenseNumber.description: The unique identifying number of the driver's driving license.

licenseCategory.description: The category of the driving license (e.g., 'A', 'B'), indicating what vehicles the driver is authorized to operate.

motoPlateNumber.description: The license plate number of the driver's motorcycle.

motoBrandModel.description: The brand and model description of the driver's motorcycle.

motoInsuranceNumber.description: The insurance policy number for the driver's motorcycle.

cooperativeName.description: The name of the cooperative or organization the driver belongs to, if any.

Ride.title: Ride
Ride.description: Records details of a specific ride request or completed trip between a passenger and a driver.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ride entity.

passengerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the passenger who requested the ride. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Ride)

driverId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (and implicitly DriverProfile) of the driver assigned to or completing the ride. This field is populated once a driver accepts the request. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N Ride)

pickupLocation.description: A GeoPoint string (e.g., 'latitude,longitude') representing the starting geographic coordinates for the ride. Can include optional human-readable address info.

destination.description: A GeoPoint string (e.g., 'latitude,longitude') representing the ending geographic coordinates for the ride. Can include optional human-readable address info.

status.description: The current status of the ride (e.g., 'requested', 'accepted', 'started', 'completed', 'rejected', 'cancelled').

fareAmount.description: The calculated fare for the completed ride.

currency.description: The currency of the fare amount (e.g., 'RWF', 'USD').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the ride request was initially created.

acceptedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the driver accepted the ride request.

startedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the ride officially started from the pickup location.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the ride was marked as completed by the driver.

cancelledAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the ride was cancelled by either passenger or driver.

cancellationReason.description: The reason provided for cancelling the ride, if applicable.

definition.description: Stores core user profile information, including their role (passenger or driver). Documents are owned by the authenticated user matching the userId parameter. Includes profilePictureUrl field.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores driver-specific details, operational status (online/offline), current location, and moto information. Documents are owned by the driver whose UID matches the driverId parameter. The 'status' and 'currentLocation' fields are denormalized here to enable efficient queries for nearby drivers without security rule get() calls.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique identifier for the driver, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Records details of ride requests and completed trips. Includes denormalized 'passengerId' and 'driverId' fields for Authorization Independence, allowing direct security rule evaluation without get() operations. 'status' field tracks ride progress.

params.name: rideId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific ride.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure for MUTAMBUKE prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling. It utilizes three top-level collections: 'userProfiles', 'driverProfiles', and 'rides'.

1.  **'/userProfiles/{userId}'**: This collection stores the core user data, including common fields like `fullName`, `phoneNumber`, `nationalId`, `email`, `role`, and `profilePictureUrl`. The `userId` in the path directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the logged-in user. This establishes clear, path-based ownership. Authorization rules for this collection are straightforward: a user can only read and write their own profile where `request.auth.uid == userId`. This design inherently supports **QAPs (Queries as Permissions)** as users can only access their specific document, making `list` operations for all user profiles unnecessary and preventing unauthorized data exposure.

2.  **'/driverProfiles/{driverId}'**: This collection extends the user profile with driver-specific information such as `drivingLicenseNumber`, `licenseCategory`, `motoPlateNumber`, `motoBrandModel`, `motoInsuranceNumber`, `cooperativeName`, and crucially, `status` and `currentLocation`. Similar to 'userProfiles', the `driverId` in the path matches the driver's Firebase Auth UID, ensuring self-ownership. The `status` and `currentLocation` fields are directly present in each document. This facilitates efficient queries by the backend Cloud Function (with elevated privileges) to find available and nearby drivers without needing to join or `get()` data from other collections. This supports **QAPs** by enabling the system to perform secure and performant geospatial queries. Direct client-side `list` access to all driver profiles is generally restricted, with driver details primarily accessed via an assigned ride.

3.  **'/rides/{rideId}'**: This collection stores all details pertinent to a ride request or completed trip. Each 'ride' document includes `passengerId` and `driverId` directly within its fields. This is the cornerstone of achieving **Authorization Independence** through denormalization. By having both `passengerId` and `driverId` present in the `Ride` document itself, Firestore Security Rules can evaluate access requests (read, write, update) using `request.auth.uid == resource.data.passengerId || request.auth.uid == resource.data.driverId` without needing to perform costly and potentially insecure `get()` operations on parent or related documents. This ensures atomic authorization checks, improves rule debuggability, and enhances performance. Furthermore, this structure perfectly supports **QAPs**: passengers can query their ride history with `where('passengerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`, and drivers can do the same with `where('driverId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. These queries are efficient and inherently secure because the `passengerId` and `driverId` fields directly define the authorized users for each ride document, ensuring that only relevant and permitted data is returned.

**Structural Segregation** is maintained by creating distinct top-level collections for different entities with varied access patterns. 'userProfiles' and 'driverProfiles' represent self-owned data, while 'rides' represents collaborative data between two specific users. This separation prevents mixing different security postures within a single collection, simplifying rule definitions. **Access Modeling** consistently applies path-based ownership for private user data and denormalized fields (`passengerId`, `driverId`) for collaborative data, minimizing rule complexity and maximizing clarity of intent.
